units.title: FASTENING TECHNIQUES USED IN TRACTOR SERVICES AND REPAIR

learning_outcomes.description: Identify Safety requirements in metal joining/fastenig

performance_criteria.description: Identify safety precautions required in metal joining and fastening
Identify the procedures involved in metal joining and fastening operations
Use the appropriate Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) when carrying out metal joining operations.
Carry out metal joining and fastening operations following Health and Safety requirements.

learning_outcomes.description: Use tools and equipment for metal joining operations

performance_criteria.description: Select tools and equipment for carrying out metal joining operations.
Ensure that the tools, equipment and PPE required are in a safe Working condition.
Ensure suitability of tools and material before use.
Use correct tools and equipment for carrying out metal joining operations

learning_outcomes.description: Carry out Metal Joining and fastening

performance_criteria.description: Prepare material and align to enable suitable joint to be achieved.
Treat lapping or meeting parts before joining.
Set up equipment for metal joining operation
Identify and remedy joint defects.
Visual inspect integrity of the joint(s).
Carry out metal joining operations
Identify common fastener failures
Protect the repaired area to prevent corrosion where applicable.
Ensure that the tools, equipment and PPE required are in a safe working condition.

learning_outcomes.description: Identify the importance of good communication

performance_criteria.description: Identify reasons why good communication is important.
Identify ways to communicate effectively.
Exhibit patience and a mild demeanour while communicating with colleagues, managers and clients.
Speak in a professional manner.
Use respectful body language even when in a bad mood or while under pressure.
Use a simple verbal means to pass on necessary information
Identify and explain symbols and signs appropriately

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate how to follow documented instructions

performance_criteria.description: Read and accurately follow steps outlined in documents.
Interpret documented instructions.
Identify specific class definitions in documented instructions.
Use information sources to address challenges in a work environment.
Communicate findings in accordance to procedure in a work environment.

learning_outcomes.description: Identify how to create documented instructions

performance_criteria.description: Identify what is needed in a documented instruction
Identify how the scope of the documented instruction is valid
Identify the importance of the documented instruction.

learning_outcomes.description: Identify personal health and hygiene

performance_criteria.description: Wear clean, smart and appropriate personal protective equipment (wears).
Work safely at all times, complying with health, safety and environmental regulations and guidelines.
Get cuts, grazes and wounds treated by the appropriate personnel.
Report any form of illness promptly to the appropriate personnel.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate Safe working Practices and Instructions

performance_criteria.description: Identify safe work practice and instructions.
Identify safety signs and symbols.
Use signs and symbols correctly.
Carry out safe work practices and instructions.
Work in accordance with health and safety best practices.

learning_outcomes.description: Identify Safety Hazards and risks

performance_criteria.description: Identify work environment hazards.
Identify various ways to avoid common workplace hazards
Identify methods to reduce the risk of work hazards.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate how to take appropriate actions during accident/injury

performance_criteria.description: Identify basic first aid equipment.
identify the benefits of first aid equipment
Maintain hygienic, safe and secure workplace.
Perform the use of safety equipment in a mobile application work environment.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate safe work habit and clean work environment

performance_criteria.description: Use safe access and exit routes in the work environment.
Have knowledge of safe work habit and clean work environment.
Dispose all wastes appropriately to designated waste facilities
Identify potential hazard(s)

learning_outcomes.description: Prevent hazards in the work place

performance_criteria.description: Identify where information about health, safety and environment in the workplace can be obtained.
Identify the types of hazard in the workplace that may occur and how to deal with them.
Demonstrate hazards that can be dealt with personally and those that should be reported to the appropriate personnel.
Demonstrate how to warn other people about potential hazard(s) and why this is important.

learning_outcomes.description: Compliance with organizational policies

performance_criteria.description: Work In line with organizational standard and structure.
Use organizational code of practice.
Apply organizational code of conduct.

learning_outcomes.description: Perform responsibilities within team

performance_criteria.description: Recognize own role and responsibilities within the team.
Perform individual tasks in line with the team rules and regulations.
Participate effectively in teamwork.

learning_outcomes.description: Develop working relationship with colleagues

performance_criteria.description: Identify the need for developing positive relationship with colleagues.
Recognize the importance of relating with other people in a way that makes them feel valued and respected.
Assist team members when required.
Report to the appropriate personnel when request/requesting for assistance falls outside area of responsibility.
Communicate information to colleagues about own work that might affect others.

units.title: REPAIR OF PLANTING AND TRANSPLANTING EQUIPMENT

learning_outcomes.description: Identify equipment for planting and transplanting

performance_criteria.description: Identify types of planting and transplanting equipment; • Seed drills • Precision planter • Transplanters
Identify the different accessories attached to planting and transplanting equipment
Identify parts of planting and transplanting equipment

learning_outcomes.description: Recognize appropriate methods for maintenance

performance_criteria.description: Identify maintenance strategy: • Daily (routine) • Preventive • Planned • Breakdown and • Shutdown.
Select appropriate maintenance strategy.
Identify protective wears in the workshop.
Identify the precautionary measures when planning for maintenance to avoid total breakdown
Recognize the benefits derived from a successful maintenance system.

learning_outcomes.description: Carryout maintenance of planting and transplanting equipment

performance_criteria.description: Identify the challenges associated with the planting and transplanting equipment
Perform the maintenance of planting and transplanting equipment,e.g.: • Planter metering device, • Drive mechanism
Use tools and equipment for a particular job.
Apply safety and work ethics throughout all maintenance activities.

learning_outcomes.description: Identify sprayers and its working principles

performance_criteria.description: Identify the working principles of common types of hand-sprayers, Knapsack sprayer, boom sprayers and crop dusters.
Identify the maintenance requirements of these spraying equipment
Identify the different types of sprayers e.g.: • Boom sprayer • Knapsack sprayer • Electro dyne sprayer and • ULV’s and • Air sprayers • Crop dusters

learning_outcomes.description: Apply safety precautions in carrying out maintenance of sprayers

performance_criteria.description: Identify common types of chemicals used in crop protection practices. E.g. pesticides
Classify the types of agro-chemicals based on time of application: • Pre- emergence • Emergence • Post-emergence
Identify environmental effects during crop protection operation
Identify the protective devices to be used; • Nose mask • Apron • Face shield • Boots • Gloves
Wear protective clothing before spraying
Ensure equipment are properly wash and dry after usage.
Store the equipment in safe place after use.

learning_outcomes.description: Perform maintenance of crop protection equipment.

performance_criteria.description: Identify type of maintenance to be carried out
Identify the maintenance requirements of crop protection equipment
Dismantle crop protection equipment
Carryout maintenance of the defective parts e.g clocked nozzles, blocked filters, faulty pumps
Re-assemble crop protection equipment
Test run the crop protection equipment

learning_outcomes.description: Select an appropriate method for repairs of hand sprayers, Knapsack, boom sprayers and crop dusters

performance_criteria.description: Identify the importance of sprayer repairs
Apply safety precautions in sprayer repairs
Use tools and equipment to carry out sprayer repair.
Apply safety in handling tools for sprayers repair

learning_outcomes.description: Carryout repairs of hand sprayers, knapsack, boom sprayers and crop dusters

performance_criteria.description: Select appropriate sprayer strategy for repairs
Identify faults to be repaired
Carry out repairs of the sprayer equipment
Identify protective wears in the workshop.
Identify the types of protective wears.
Apply the safety rules in the workshop

units.title: REPAIR OF HARVESTING AND THRESHING EQUIPMENT

learning_outcomes.description: Identify the working principles of harvesting/threshing equipment

performance_criteria.description: Identify types of harvesting and threshing equipment used for harvesting forage, grain and cutting grass
Identify the working principles for: • Harvesting forage • Grain • Cutting grass
Identify the challenges associated with the working principles of harvesting forage, Grain and grass.
Compare the different processes of harvesting: • Forage • Grain • Grass

learning_outcomes.description: Identify adjustment and maintenance procedure of harvesters/threshers.

performance_criteria.description: Distinguish between the operations of a harvester and threshers.
Carry out adjustments of harvesters and threshers; cylinder concave clearance, fan air intake, volume adjustment
Identify adjustment and maintenance procedures of harvesters and threshers.

learning_outcomes.description: Recognize appropriate method for repairs of harvesting/threshing machine

performance_criteria.description: Identify the importance of repairs of harvesting/threshing machine
Identify the appropriate tool for repairs of harvesting/threshing machine
Identify the required repairs and carry out the repairs
Use tools and equipment in line with manufacturer’s specification.
Apply safety and work ethics throughout the repair activities.

learning_outcomes.description: Carryout Repairs of harvesting/threshing machine

performance_criteria.description: Perform repairs of harvesting/threshing machine.
Apply safety in storing and securing tools.
Store the parts and tools appropriately
Perform all repairs activities following manufacturers' instructions, your workplace procedures and Health, Safety and Environment legal requirements

learning_outcomes.description: Carry out maintenance on Harvesting/Threshing machine.

performance_criteria.description: Perform repairs of harvesting/threshing machine.
Apply safety in storing and securing harvesting/threshing tools.
Store the parts and tools appropriately
Perform all repair activities following manufacturers' instructions, your workplace procedures and Health, Safety and Environment legal requirements

units.title: REPAIR OF CROP PROCESSING AND STORAGE EQUIPMENT

learning_outcomes.description: Identify crop processing and storage equipment

performance_criteria.description: Identify types of crop processing and storage equipment e.g.,: • Crop Processing: threshers, hammer mills, dryers, washing machine; • Storage: rhumbus, Silos
Identify the functions of crop processing and storage equipment
Identify the working principles of the equipment mentioned above

learning_outcomes.description: Identify faulty crop processing and storage equipment

performance_criteria.description: Select repairs and maintenance methods of crop processing and storage equipment according to daily (routine) maintenance and preventative maintenance
Identify parts needed to carry out the repairs and maintenance
Procure parts required for repairs and maintenance
State the functions of each of the repairs and maintenance method.
Identify the precautions and planning techniques for shutdown maintenance.

learning_outcomes.description: Carryout repair and maintenance of crop processing and storage equipment

performance_criteria.description: Identify the faults in crop processing and storage equipment
Select appropriate tools for the repair and maintenance of crop processing and storage equipment.
Perform repair and maintenance of the crop processing and storage equipment
Identify protective wears for the repair and maintenance of crop processing and storage equipment.
Test run crop processing and storage equipment

learning_outcomes.description: Identify types of maintenance of farm tractors.

performance_criteria.description: Identify types of maintenance to be carried out on farm tractors. e.g.: • Routine maintenance • Preventive maintenance
Differentiate between preventive and routine maintenance of farm tractors.
Identify operators manuals on maintenance

learning_outcomes.description: Identify the different preventive maintenance of Tractors

performance_criteria.description: Identify preventive maintenance, e.g. • Weekly • Daily.
Identify parts of tractors that require preventive maintenance
Identify tools used in preventive maintenance

learning_outcomes.description: Identify the different routine maintenance

performance_criteria.description: Identity routine maintenance, e.g: • 50hrs • 150hrs • 250hrs • 500hrs
Identify tractor parts that require routine maintenance
Identify tools used in routine maintenance

learning_outcomes.description: Carry out periodic maintenance

performance_criteria.description: Identify types of maintenance to be carried out e.g. see 3.1 above
Select tools use for specific maintenance
Perform the maintenance required.
Test run the tractors
Record the maintenance in service book

learning_outcomes.description: Identify Workshop Records

performance_criteria.description: Identify various records used in a workshop: • Receipts • Invoices • Inventory
Identify procedures for preparing various records used in the workshop.
Apply procedures for safe and proper financial records keeping.

learning_outcomes.description: Identify Workshop job Related Records

performance_criteria.description: Identify reasons for keeping job related records.
Identify various job related records used in the workshop: • job cards • requisition forms • purchase order forms • workshop delivery forms, etc.
Identify procedures for preparing various job related records used in the workshop.
Identify procedures for safe and proper job related records keeping.
Observe equipment and material movement.
Identify workshop procurement procedures for materials, tools in workshop.
Follow procedures for procuring materials, tools and equipment using: • Manuals and reference materials • Requests and approvals • Order placements • Reception of goods and items • Payments • Storage

units.title: TRACTOR WHEEL BALANCING AND ALIGNMENT OPERATIONS

learning_outcomes.description: Understand Wheel balancing operations

performance_criteria.description: Differentiate between wheel alignment and balancing.
Define the following wheel balancing terms: • Dynamic unbalance • Static unbalance • Toe-in & Toe-out
State the effects of: • Under inflation of tyres • Over inflation of tyres

learning_outcomes.description: Identify Wheel balancing tools and equipment

performance_criteria.description: Identify various wheel alignment tools/equipment correctly.
Ensure that measuring and adjustment tools and equipment are safe and in good working condition.
Store tools and equipment according to manufacturer’s specification.

learning_outcomes.description: Carry out Pre- balancing checks

performance_criteria.description: List the step-by-step procedures for pre-balancing checks
Conduct wheel balancing pre checks operations.
Apply Health, Safety and Environment requirements

learning_outcomes.description: Perform Wheel balancing procedures

performance_criteria.description: Use suitable personal protective equipment
Conduct wheel balancing pre- checks operations.
Identify the various valves on the tyre, e.g.: • Rim size • Width • Tyre classification etc.
Ensure final adjustment and settings
Complete all four wheel balancing operations within the agreed timescale.

learning_outcomes.description: Perform post balancing checks

performance_criteria.description: Identify the purpose of post- balancing checks.
List the step-by-step procedures for post-balancing checks.
Carry out post wheel balancing checks to ensure conformity to specifications.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand Wheel Alignment Operations

performance_criteria.description: Explain wheel alignment
State the purpose of the steering and suspension system.
State reasons for tyre wear.
State the function of the following alignment terms: • Castor • Camber • King Pin Inclination • Steering Angle Inclination • Toe-in & Toe-out.

learning_outcomes.description: Identify Wheel Alignment Tools and Equipment

performance_criteria.description: Identify various wheel alignment tools/equipment correctly.
Ensure that measuring and adjustment tools and equipment are safe and in good working condition.
Store tools and equipment according to manufacturer’s specification.
Identify the purpose of pre- alignment checks
List the step-by-step procedures for pre-alignment checks

Conduct all wheel alignment pre checks and wheel alignment operations following the correct technical data the manufacturer’s instructions your workplace procedure Health, Safety and Environment requirements.

learning_outcomes.description: Perform Wheel Alignment Procedures

performance_criteria.description: Use suitable personal protective equipment and motor vehicle coverings throughout all wheel alignment operations.

Conduct all wheel alignment pre checks and four wheel alignment operations following the correct technical data the manufacturer’s instructions Workplace procedure Health, Safety and environment requirements.
Perform adjustment and settings within the recommended tolerance levels.
Carry out wheel alignment operations within the agreed timescale.

learning_outcomes.description: Carryout Alignment Post Checks

performance_criteria.description: State the reasons for carrying out post-alignment checks.
List the step-by-step procedures for post-alignment checks.
Perform post wheel alignment checks.
